location.description: Where the error occurred, e.g. 'body.items[3].tags' or 'path.thing-id'

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
examples: Property foo is required but is missing.

errors.description: Optional list of individual error details

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. This value should not change between occurrences of the error.

type.description: A URI reference to human-readable documentation for the error.

attributes.description: Custom node attributes (internal ES attributes filtered out).

coordinating.description: Whether the node is a coordinating-only node.

current_master.description: Whether this node is the elected master.

data.description: Whether the node has a data role.

disk.description: Disk usage (null when filesystem stats are unavailable).

host.description: Node host.

ingest.description: Whether the node has the ingest role.

$schema.description: A URL to the JSON Schema for this object.

number_of_nodes.description: Number of nodes in the cluster.

shard_allocation.description: Whether shard allocation is enabled (cluster.routing.allocation.enable == all).

data_stream.description: Data stream name when this index is a backing index.

shards.description: Routing table shards grouped by node name ('unassigned' for unassigned shards).

special.description: Whether the index is dot-prefixed (system index).

unhealthy.description: Whether any shard is not STARTED.

post.description: Returns all index aliases flattened into one list.
Applies a batch of alias add/remove actions.
Runs _analyze with the given analyzer and returns the produced tokens.
Runs _analyze against the analyzer of the given field and returns the produced tokens.
Returns analyzer names defined in the index settings.
Returns text fields of the index that can be analyzed.
Returns names of open indices available for analysis.
Proxies the given Elasticsearch _cat API and returns its JSON output verbatim.
Returns the current index and node names plus the cluster name, used by the UI to detect cluster topology changes.
Returns the cluster settings (persistent and transient) as reported by Elasticsearch.
Updates the cluster settings with the given document.
Checks connectivity by fetching _cluster/health of the target host.

get.description: Returns the names of the Elasticsearch hosts configured in Cerebro.

post.description: Returns the mappings and settings of an existing index, used to prefill the create-index form.
Feature-flagged document insert/update for one Elasticsearch index.
Read-only, feature-flagged document browser for one Elasticsearch index.
Returns the index settings in flat format, including defaults.
Returns the cluster health document (verbatim from _cluster/health), extended with a "username" field when authentication is enabled.
Returns one entry per node with roles, heap, disk, CPU and uptime information.
Returns the cluster dashboard payload: health, indices with shard routing, and nodes.
Returns stats (or recovery info) for one shard of an index on a given node.
